channel.title: BBC Sport
channel.copyright: Copyright: (C) British Broadcasting Corporation, see https://www.bbc.co.uk/usingthebbc/terms-of-use/#15metadataandrssfeeds for terms and conditions of reuse.

item.title: Spurs lifeline or good step for West Ham - who's favourite for drop?
item.description: With five games remaining, BBC Sport looks at which side is most likely to take the third relegation spot.

item.title: Why Wolves' relegation had been coming after slow decline
item.description: Wolves have been relegated from the Premier League after just three wins in their first 33 games of the season.

item.title: Who has made Troy's Premier League team of the week?
item.description: After every round of Premier League matches this season, Troy Deeney gives us his team of the week. Do you agree with his choices?

item.title: West Ham earn valuable point at London rivals Crystal Palace
item.description: West Ham edge a point clearer of the relegation zone as they share a goalless draw with Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Rose agrees to succeed Iraola at Bournemouth
item.description: Marco Rose agrees to take over at Bournemouth when manager Andoni Iraola leaves at the end of the season.

item.title: 'Worst I've seen' - Leicester face relegation again
item.description: BBC Sport takes a look at what Leicester City need in their seemingly doomed fight for Championship survival.

item.title: Rosenior feels '100%' support from Chelsea owners
item.description: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior says he feels "100%" support from the club's ownership despite a run of four league defeats without scoring.

item.title: Konate says he is close to signing new Liverpool deal
item.description: Ibrahima Konate says he is "close to an agreement" with Liverpool over a new contract and there is a "big chance" he will stay.

item.title: Lukaku holds positive clear-the-air talks with Napoli
item.description: Romelu Lukaku will continue his rehabilitation from injury in Belgium after holding clear-the-air talks with Napoli.

item.title: Should Gabriel have been sent off for Haaland clash?
item.description: Gabriel pushed his head towards Erling Haaland's face yet escaped with only a booking. Why did the VAR not intervene for a red card?

item.title: Gordon will cost Bayern Munich £75m - Tuesday's gossip
item.description: Newcastle will sell Anthony Gordon if Bayern Munich match their £75m valuation, Manchester United keeping close eye on Aurelien Tchouameni, Yan Diomande top target to replace Mohamed Salah at Liverpool, plus more.

item.title: 'It will go all the way' - Nuno on relegation battle after draw at Palace
item.description: West Ham manager Nuno Espirito Santo belives the Premier League relegation battle "will go all the way" after his side's 0-0 draw with Crystal Palace at Selhurst Park.

item.title: Palace not clinical enough in West Ham draw - Glasner
item.description: Crystal Palace manager Oliver Glasner says his side were "not clinical enough" in their 0-0 Premier League draw with West Ham at Selhurst Park.

item.title: How Haaland and Gabriel went head-to-head in 'old school battle'
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Wayne Rooney and Danny Murphy discuss how "two top players" in Erling Haaland and Gabriel went head-to-head in Manchester City's 2-1 victory against Arsenal.

item.title: Bayern beat Stuttgart to seal 35th Bundesliga title
item.description: Bayern Munich come from behind to secure a 4-2 victory against Stuttgart and win their 35th German Bundesliga title with four games to spare.

item.title: Rooney and Murphy predict Premier League title outcome
item.description: Match of the Day pundits Wayne Rooney and Danny Murphy discuss Arsenal and Manchester City's remaining fixtures in the Premier League title race and have their say on who they think will lift this year's trophy.

item.title: 'By chance or choice, they have clicked' - why Man City will be champions
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Danny Murphy explains why he thinks Manchester City will beat Arsenal to the Premier League title.

item.title: Redesigned ball could reduce heading impact - scientists
item.description: Scientists believe footballs could be designed to reduce the impact of heading after new research discovered the action caused a previously unreported pressure wave energy transfer to the brain.

item.title: Lennon cup final reunion for what could be O'Neill swansong
item.description: Having met 30 years ago and shared success at Leicester City and Celtic, Martin O'Neill and Neil Lennon are ready to good head-to-head for the first time in May's Scottish Cup final.

item.title: Record-breaking Bayern win Bundesliga - now for the Treble
item.description: Free-scoring Bayern Munich swept to the German title for the 35th time. Now their sights turn towards completing the Treble.

item.title: Forest hero Gibbs-White plunges summer suitors Spurs into deeper danger
item.description: Morgan Gibbs-White's hat-trick for Nottingham Forest serves as another painful reminder of Tottenham's failed pursuit of the striker last summer.

item.title: Will St Johnstone secure title in front of BBC cameras?
item.description: St Johnstone travel to Dunfermline on Tuesday with a chance to secure the Scottish Championship title and promotion live on the BBC Scotland channel.

item.title: Are Man City now title favourites after win over Arsenal?
item.description: Erling Haaland's winning goal, the explosion of noise, the reaction at full-time - are Man City now favourites to win the Premier League title?

item.title: Police fire rubber bullets and detain dozens as Paraguay derby abandoned
item.description: Six police officers were hurt, while dozens of fans were detained causing a Paraguay first division game to be abandoned after 29 minutes.

item.title: Dream derby result for Liverpool fans but huge summer ahead for Slot
item.description: Liverpool's 100th-minute winner at Everton moves the Reds closer to a Champions League spot - but issues still remain.

item.title: Arsenal fans must be better to help team in title hunt - Rooney
item.description: Ex-England and Manchester United captain Wayne Rooney says Arsenal fans "need to be better" in supporting the team in their faltering Premier League title bid.

item.title: Champions League almost sewn up but race for Europe tighter than ever
item.description: Scrolling down the Premier League table, the Europa League and Europa Conference League spots are still anyone's guess.

item.title: St Mirren's Gogic hits out at own fans over keeper abuse
item.description: St Mirren defender Alex Gogic criticises the "disgraceful" and "disgusting" abuse goalkeeper Ryan Mullen received from his own fans after the 6-2 Scottish Cup semi-final defeat by Celtic.

item.title: How Liverpool's improved creative play helped them beat Everton
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Danny Murphy analyses how Liverpool's performance "got better" from a "creative" and "footballing aspect" as The Reds secured a 2-1 victory at Everton in the Merseyside Derby.

item.title: Man City beat Arsenal to close gap in title race
item.description: Erling Haaland and Rayan Cherki strike as Manchester City beat league leaders Arsenal to close the gap to three points with a game in hand and pile more pressure on Mikel Arteta's side.

item.title: Arteta rues missed Arsenal chances in loss at Man City
item.description: Arsenal manager Mikel Arteta says his side had "the best chances in the game" against Manchester City.

item.title: Van Dijk's 100th-minute winner settles Merseyside derby
item.description: Liverpool captain Virgil van Dijk scores a 100th-minute winner to secure an important victory against Everton in the first Merseyside Derby at The Hill Dickinson Stadium.

item.title: Villa score late to beat Sunderland in seven-goal thriller
item.description: Tammy Abraham's late winner helps Aston Villa survive a late Sunderland fightback and give Unai Emery's side a crucial 4-3 victory in their quest for Champions League football next season.

item.title: Will lack of a ruthless match-winner cost Arsenal the title?
item.description: Arsenal's lack of killer instinct is looking like it could damage their chance of a first league title in 22 years.

item.title: Quiz: Name all the clubs who have won Bundesliga title
item.description: Thirteen clubs have won a German Bundesliga title since the league began in 1963 - can you name them all?

item.title: Ten days to decide second place in the Championship?
item.description: Four clubs are locked in a battle to secure the second promotion spot in the Championship.

item.title: Give our Premier League top assisters quiz a go
item.description: The list for most assists in a single Premier League season is so full of legends it inspired us to build this quiz.

item.title: Why evergreen Forrest is Celtic's enduring influencer
item.description: Veteran winger James Forrest remains Celtic's constant, the great survivor and the enduring influencer, writes Tom English.

item.title: Coventry's Lampard and Boro's Hackney win at EFL Awards
item.description: Coventry boss Frank Lampard and Middlesbrough midfielder Hayden Hackney are named the EFL Championship manager and player of the season.

item.title: IFA submits planning application for National Football Centre
item.description: The Irish FA reveals that it has formally submitted a planning application for their new National Football Centre on a 50-acre site at Galgorm, near Ballymena.

item.title: Gibbs-White hat-trick earns Forest win over Burnley
item.description: Morgan Gibbs-White's hat-trick helps Nottingham Forest secure a vital 4-1 comeback victory in their bid for Premier League survival, and leaves Burnley on the brink of relegation.

item.title: 'We're not top yet' - Guardiola cautious despite win
item.description: Manchester City manager Pep Guardiola says his side are still not top of the league and their remaining fixtures are going to be "so difficult" after The Blues beat league leaders Arsenal to go three points behind with a game in hand.

item.title: How Gibbs-White made a 'huge difference' in Forest's win against Burnley
item.description: Match of the Day pundit Danny Murphy discusses how Vítor Pereira's half-time changes "benefitted" Morgan Gibbs-White as the England international made a "huge difference" in Nottingham Forest's 4-1 comeback victory against Burnley.

item.title: First 'daylight' offside goal scored in Canadian Premier League
item.description: Pacific FC forward Alejandro Diaz scores against Halifax Wanderers in the Canadian Premier League, the first instance of a "daylight offside" situation resulting in a goal.

item.title: Derby win for the fans after tough season - Slot
item.description: Liverpool manager Arne Slot says his side's late derby victory against Everton is "definitely a day for the fans to be happy about" after The Reds' recent quarter-final defeats in the FA Cup and Champions League.

item.title: Protests and another costly loss - pressure mounts on Rosenior's Chelsea
item.description: Chelsea head coach Liam Rosenior admits his side face a "mountain to climb" as their campaign unravels at a crucial juncture following defeat by Manchester United.

item.title: Spurs condemn 'vile' racist abuse aimed at Danso
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur have reported "vile, dehumanising" abuse towards defender Kevin Danso to the police.

item.title: Cardiff's priorities as they plan for Championship
item.description: From transfers to contract renewals, BBC Sport Wales looks at Cardiff City's priorities as they prepare for life back in the Championship.

item.title: Wild celebrations to verge of tears - time running out for Spurs
item.description: Scenes of celebration turned to bitter disappointment as Tottenham's hopes of avoiding relegation from the Premier League were dealt a devastating blow against Brighton.

item.title: Howe's biggest Newcastle challenge yet as questions grow on his future
item.description: Newcastle are languishing in 14th place in the Premier League following defeat by Bournemouth. Will manager Eddie Howe turn it around?

item.title: Carrick silences doubters as Man Utd close on Champions League
item.description: Manchester United beat Chelsea to move closer to a Champions League return and silence the growing Michael Carrick doubters.

item.title: Can Leeds feel safe from relegation after back-to-back wins?
item.description: Leeds United are eight points above the Premier League drop zone after back-to-back wins - so can they feel safe from relegation?

item.title: How Hampton's heroics bailed out Lionesses in win against Iceland
item.description: Goalkeeper Hannah Hampton says she just "did my job" in pulling off three crucial late saves as England held on to beat Iceland 1-0.

item.title: Iraola bowing out in style - where will he go next?
item.description: Any suggestion of distraction following Andoni Iraola's decision to leave Bournemouth was firmly dismissed by his players in their statement victory against Newcastle.

item.title: How Lampard's own revival ended Coventry's 25-year wait
item.description: Frank Lampard and Coventry City are back in the big time. This is how the former England international guided the Sky Blues to the Premier League.

item.title: One of Arsenal's greatest unsung heroes - Keown's tribute to Manninger
item.description: Former Arsenal defender Martin Keown pays tribute to his old friend and former Gunners team-mate Alex Manninger, who has died at the age of 48.

item.title: How Coventry earned Premier League return after 25 years
item.description: After a quarter of a century out of the Premier League, groundshares and administration, Frank Lampard has led Coventry City back to the top flight.

item.title: All done deals since February, including deadline day
item.description: Check out the significant signings and departures in the Premier League, Scottish Premiership, EFL and Women's Super League.

item.title: Why did Bayern Munich players wear cockatoo T-shirts?
item.description: Bayern Munich's players celebrated their 35th German top-flight title on Sunday with T-shirts displaying a cockatoo

item.title: How many points do you need to stay in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks into the number of points needed to avoid relegation from the Premier League

item.title: How are the Scottish Premiership post-split fixtures decided?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership split has again been a controversial talking point, so why does it exist and why is it so complicated?

item.title: Who can still receive a yellow card suspension in the Premier League?
item.description: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team looks at who is currently nearing a suspension in the Premier League.

item.title: How does Scottish Premiership split work?
item.description: The Scottish Premiership title race has been a hot topic all season and it's about to become even more interesting with the league's split.

item.title: Have all home nations ever played in same World Cup?
item.description: With England and Scotland already qualified for the 2026 World Cup, Wales and Northern Ireland both face European qualifiers in March.

item.title: All you need to know about 2027 Women's World Cup
item.description: Our Ask Me Anything team take a look at everything you need to know for the 2027 Women's World Cup.

item.title: Tavernier confirms no offer yet of new Rangers contract
item.description: Rangers captain James Tavernier says "time will tell" whether he will be at the club next season, but he is relishing the three-way Scottish title race.

item.title: Highlights from crucial weekend games in SPFL
item.description: Watch highlights as Arbroath secure a promotion play-off spot in the Championship, Inverness Caledonian Thistle move closer to the League 1 title, while Clyde secure a promotion play-off spot in League 2.

item.title: 'Melbourne's Rawlins signs Dundee United deal' - gossip
item.description: Dundee United reportedly agree the purchase of Melbourne Victory's Joshua Rawlins, while Tom McIntyre's spell on loan to Aberdeen comes to an end.

item.title: FC Basel postpone fixture after stadium fire
item.description: FC Basel's Swiss Super League match against FC Thun is postponed after a fire causes "significant damage" to Basel's St Jakob-Park stadium.

item.title: Spalletti signs new Juventus contract amid Italy links
item.description: Juventus manager Luciano Spalletti signs a two-year contract extension with the club, effectively ruling him out of a return to the Italy national team.

item.title: How Lens embraced underdog status to become PSG's title rivals
item.description: Lens have pushed PSG closer than anyone in Ligue 1 this season and, with the sides still to meet again, still hold an outside shot at the title.

item.title: Legendary manager Lucescu dies five days after resigning as Romania coach
item.description: Legendary manager Mircea Lucescu dies aged 80, five days after stepping down as Romania's head coach.

item.title: Lessons to be learned as Wales chase top spot
item.description: Wales kept their qualifying campaign on track with victory in Albania, but better is required if they are to reach a first Women's World Cup.

item.title: What did we learn from McArdle's first two games in charge?
item.description: Following two impressive wins against Malta, BBC Sport NI looks at what we've learnt from Michael McArdle's first two games in charge.

item.title: Can you name every Women's World Cup host?
item.description: Challenge yourself with our Women's World Cup hosts quiz.

item.title: When football turned to penalties to end 'cruel' system
item.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.

item.title: Frank's downfall: Inside his struggle to get players and fans onboard
item.description: Thomas Frank has been sacked as Tottenham manager after just eight months in charge. BBC Sport's Sami Mokbel takes a closer look at his tenure.

item.title: Cages, crushes and stabbings - is European away safety getting worse?
item.description: BBC Sport explores the safety issues fans face when following their team across Europe.

item.title: The great number nine decline - where have England's strikers gone?
item.description: BBC Sport takes an in-depth look at why England lack centre-forward options behind Harry Kane - a far cry from when the likes of Les Ferdinand only won 17 caps.

item.title: Captain, player, manager, Mister, number one - Davids at Barnet
item.description: BBC Sport remembers Edgar Davids' 15 months as Barnet player-manager in League Two and the Conference.

item.title: From farming village to Liverpool icon - the Salah story
item.description: BBC Sport visits Egypt to discover what Mohamed Salah means to the people of the football-mad country, and how a small boy from humble beginnings became a national icon.

item.title: Napoli 'smell blood' & target Premier League's discarded stars
item.description: After Scott McTominay was a hit, Napoli went back to the Premier League for Kevin de Bruyne, could more players follow them to Serie A?
